A test file for strict mode evaluation of postfix increment in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code snippet demonstrates a simple operation that increments the global `eval` function by one. The use of `"use strict"` at the beginning of the file enforces strict mode, which helps catch common coding errors and prevents the use of certain JavaScript features. However, in strict mode, attempting to modify the `eval` function results in a syntax error, as `eval` is a reserved keyword in JavaScript. This code serves as an example of strict mode behavior and its restrictions on modifying built-in JavaScript functions.
